text: REST APIs are a popular way to build web services that allow different applications to communicate. When designing API endpoints, it is important to use clear and consistent naming, such as `/users` or `/products`, to represent resources. HTTP status codes, like 200 for success or 404 for not found, help clients understand the result of their requests. Good documentation is essential; it should explain how to use each endpoint, what parameters are accepted, and what responses to expect. Finally, versioning your API, for example by including `/v1/` in the URL, ensures that changes do not break existing users. Following these best practices makes your API reliable and easy to use.
